title: nimiq-stack manifest (v1)
description: One per Nimiq fleet app at repo root. Declares the app's stack, styling, on-chain settlement path, deploy target, and config posture so `nq align` can grade each axis against the canonical fleet baseline using the same clean / safe-drift / risky-fail vocabulary as `nq audit`. The load-bearing axis is SETTLEMENT: the @nimiq/core light client (Client.create / @nimiq/core/web) is broken on our hosts, so chain reads must go through the rpc-block-scan pattern and core must be offline-crypto-only.

chainApp.description: True if the app settles real NIM on-chain (subject to settlement + styling parity). False for read-only / informational apps (use --no-chain).

exempt.description: True for apps intentionally off the canonical stack (e.g. nimiq.tech, nimiq-ads, gateflo). Exempt apps are REPORTED by nq align but never failed.

source.description: nimiq-ui = @nimiq/style + nq-* component classes (canonical). Anything else fails for a non-exempt chainApp.

pattern.description: rpc-block-scan (canonical) | mock | noop | light-client (FORBIDDEN — broken on our hosts)

lib.description: nimiq-settlement (shared pkg) | inline (src/lib/chain.ts, migrating to the shared pkg) | none

coreRole.description: offline-crypto-only (canonical: @nimiq/core used only for key/tx signing, never Client.create) | none

fileSizeGuard.description: max source-file line count enforced in CI, or false

canonicalVersion.description: semver of the canonical fleet baseline this app last aligned to
